title: Kiln Furnace
description: Adds a new furnace type for decorative blocks and charcoal.
body:
Kiln adds a new functional block designed for vanilla-plus play. It integrates cleanly with the existing crafting loop and is intended to feel like a natural extension of Minecraft progression.

The Kiln works similarly to the Smoker and Blast Furnace, but for decorative blocks and charcoal. The Kiln can only fire certain items/blocks, but does so faster than a traditional furnace.

Recipes
- Clay ball to brick
- Netherrack to nether brick
- Sand and red sand to glass
- Clay to terracotta
- Dyed terracotta to glazed terracotta
- Cobblestone to stone, stone to smooth stone
- Cobbled deepslate to deepslate
- Cracked variants of multiple blocks from their respective, uncracked variants.
- Sandstone, red sandstone, and quartz block to their respective smooth variants.
- Charcoal from all current (1.21.11) vanilla logs, wood, stripped logs, and stripped wood (not including nether wood types and bamboo).

Features
- New Kiln block
- Clean, vanilla-style texture and UI behavior
- Built for Fabric on Minecraft 1.21.11

Requirements
- Fabric Loader
- Fabric API

Installation
1. Install Fabric Loader for 1.21.11
2. Install Fabric API (matching 1.21.11)
3. Drop the Kiln `.jar` into your `mods` folder

Compatibility
- Should be compatible with most vanilla-plus modpacks
- No worldgen changes
